---
title: 'Mood Note'
date: '2020-04-04T06:20:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7631570032762672293'
blogger_filename: '/2020/04/mood-note.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
---

<div class="WordSection1">  <p class="MsoNormal">I’ve said “I Love You” and “I Miss You” many times in the last few months. She has not. The closest she came is “I do love you, maybe not in the way you wanted to expected but I do love you.” Which is another way of saying she doesn’t,   not romantically. Not the way I do. I don’t know why I pursue her still. Why I still try. I won the debate. We both thought the other would leave first, she left first, I won. There’s no reason for me to hold on. So why do I? Because I’ve met exactly TWO women   in my whole life like her, Flavia and Shelley. Different on the outside, but that heart for God… I guess I have to decide who I’m going to be, which Self I’m going to be, before I can be allowed a partner that aligns with that self.</p></div>
